attribution: Structure inspired by aimodes.ai (Dr. Mark Keith, BYU). Definitions and thresholds are canvas-toolbox's own working drafts for development only — NOT Keith's validated instrument. Do not represent output as his model.

tiers.label: Passivity
tiers.description: Student outsources the thinking; AI produces, student receives.

tiers.label: Partnership
tiers.description: Student and AI think together; shared cognitive load.

tiers.label: Agency
tiers.description: Student drives and checks the AI; the AI serves the student's direction.

modes.label: Oracle
modes.definition: Student asks for a fact or answer and takes it as-is, with no reasoning exchange.
signals: no attempt to reason through it themselves

modes.label: Production Assistant
modes.definition: Student delegates production of an artifact and takes the output to use directly.
signals: receives a finished artifact to submit or use

modes.label: Tutor
modes.definition: Student asks the AI to explain or teach a concept so that the student understands it.
signals: asks follow-ups that show they are trying to learn, not just get output

modes.label: Collaborative Problem-Solver
modes.definition: Student and AI iterate jointly on a problem — the student proposes, the AI refines, the student pushes back and contributes.
signals: 'here's my idea, what do you think'
student contributes substance, not just prompts

modes.label: Verification Agent
modes.definition: Student has done the work and uses the AI to check their own reasoning or find their own errors.
signals: 'here is my solution — is it correct?'
the student's own work is present and central

modes.label: Creative Expander
modes.definition: Student directs the AI to broaden the option space beyond their own ideas, then selects among the results themselves.

modes.label: Critical Challenger
modes.definition: Student invites adversarial pressure — asks the AI to critique, argue against, or find holes in the student's own position.

modes.label: Problem Setter
modes.definition: Student frames and scopes the problem itself, sets the agenda, and uses the AI as an instrument toward a goal the student owns.
signals: defines what to solve and why
sequences sub-tasks toward a student-owned objective
